data.title: ZJK Industrial ramps up production to support Nvidia’s B40 project
data.description:
• ZJK Industrial (NASDAQ:ZJK) on Thursday announced that it is ramping up production to meet growing demand from Nvidia’s B40 project.
  • Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is advancing the development of a customized AI accelerator, the B40 chip, specifically for the Chinese market. 
  • Mass production of the B40, which is based on Nvidia’s Blackwell architecture and targets the mid-to-high-end market segment, could be started as early as June 2025.
  • “As a supplier to Nvidia, we see tremendous growth potential in this project, given the market size and surging demand in the AI sector. We expect this initiative to contribute significant year-over-year revenue growth," Ning Ding, CEO of ZJK said.

data.title: Stock index futures rise sharply as traders digest labor reports
data.description:
Stock index futures rose sharply on Thursday, as investors assessed the latest labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.86%.

The initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week. 

Productivity growth was revised lower in the final Q1 estimates, and labor costs extended their climb.

The U.S. services sector leads in May job cuts, according to the Challenger Report.

Wall Street on Wednesday closed little changed, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

"Markets put in a strong performance yesterday, after a weak batch of US data led to a massive rally for U.S.Treasuries, which in turn supported risk assets," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Of course, even with the rally, the data still raised fears that the U.S. economy lost steam into May. That started off with the ADP’s report of private payrolls, which was the softest in over two years, Allen added. 

The main event in terms of labor data—the monthly employment reading—will land on Friday.

data.title: Vince McMahon sells 1.6M TKO shares for $250M - filing
data.description:
According to a Securities and Exchange filing disclosed by TKO Group (NYSE:TKO) on Thursday, Endeavor Operating Company (EOC) has purchased 1,579,080 Class A shares from Vince McMahon at $158.32 per share for a total transaction value of $250M.

The transaction concluded on June 4, 2025. EOC used available working capital to fund the purchases, the SEC filing shows.

EOC now indirectly owns 4,158,517 shares of TKO Group. 

SEC filing

data.title: GEO Group to sell Lawton, Oklahoma, facility for $312M
data.description:
GEO Group (NYSE:GEO) agreed to sell its Lawton, Oklahoma, correctional facility to the Oklahoma Department of Corrections for $312M, the company said on Thursday.

The company plans to use the net proceeds from the sale to pay down debt and for corporate purposes.

"The sale of our company-owned Lawton Correctional Facility is expected to be a significant deleveraging event for our company," said GEO Executive Chairman George C. Zoley. "We believe that this important transaction is representative of the intrinsic value of our company-owned facilities, which total more than 52,000 beds."

GEO (NYSE:GEO) stock fell 1.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

The transaction is expected to close on July 25, 2025, and expects the facilities operations to simultaneously transfer to the Oklahoma Department of Corrections.

In its Q1 earnings, GEO said it plans to reduce its total debt by ~$150M-$175M in 2025, bringing its total debt to $1.54B. At the end of Q1, its total debt stood at $1.68B, and its net leverage was ~3.78x adjusted EBITDA.

data.title: Cracker Barrel hikes operating profit forecast even as retail traffic slips
data.description:
Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (NASDAQ:CBRL) reported its fourth straight quarter of positive comparable restaurant sales, though a 3.8% drop in retail traffic limited overall net sales growth in the fiscal third quarter.

With expectations for easing labor and commodity costs, the company raised its full-year adjusted EBITDA outlook, giving a modest boost to shares in Thursday’s premarket trading.

The company now expects hourly wage inflation in the mid 2% range versus prior expectation of 2% to 3% labor wage inflation. Commodity inflation in expected to be in the mid 2% range compared to previous outlook for 2% to 3%. Accordingly, adjusted EBITDA is now expected to be between $215M to $225M from $210M to $220M, previously. Total revenue guidance was left unchanged at $3.45B to $3.50B versus $3.47B estimates.

For the quarter ended May 2, the restaurant/retail store chain earned an adjusted profit of $0.58 per share which was down from $0.88 last year but $0.37 better than expected. Adjusted EBITDA increased 0.4% to $48.1%, or 5.9% of total revenue.

For the restaurant segment, comparable restaurant sales were up 1%, driving revenue to $661M, while retail comparable sales were down 3.8%, resulting in a 2.7% decline in total revenue to $141.7M. Accordingly, net sales were up by just 0.5% to $821.15M, $1.3M shy of expectations.

Along with FQ3 results, the company also announced the distribution of an unchanged quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share, payable on August 13 to shareholders of record as of July 18.

data.title: Toro Non-GAAP EPS of $1.42 beats by $0.02, revenue of $1.32B misses by $30M
data.description:
• Toro press release (NYSE:TTC): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.42 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $1.32B (-2.2% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • For fiscal 2025, management now expects total company net sales to be in the range of flat to down 3% and adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $4.15 to $4.30 vs $4.31 consensus.

data.title: Brown-Forman slides after warning about 'challenging' operating environment
data.description:
Brown-Forman Corporation (NYSE:BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B) reported sales fell 7.3% year-over-year in FQ4 to $894 million. Sales were down 3% for the quarter that ended on April 30 on an organic basis. Gross profit was down 10% during the quarter. Operating income was down 45%. EPS was reported at $0.31 vs. $0.34 consensus and $0.56 a year ago.

"While our results did not meet our long-term growth aspirations, we made important progress in an exceptionally challenging macroeconomic environment," stated CEO Lawson Whiting. "Looking ahead to fiscal 2026, we expect continued headwinds. Still, we are confident that with agility, innovation, and a clear focus on execution, we are well positioned to navigate uncertainty and unlock new opportunities for sustainable long-term growth," he added.

Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B) anticipates the operating environment for FY26 will be challenging, with low visibility due to macroeconomic and geopolitical volatility as the company faces headwinds from consumer uncertainty, the potential impact from currently unknown tariffs, and lower non-branded sales of used barrels. In terms of guidance, organic net sales are seen declining in the low-single digit range. Organic operating income is anticipated to decline in the low-single digit range.

The company's stable of brands includes Jack Daniel's Tennessee Whiskey, Jack Daniel's Ready-to-Drinks, Jack Daniel's Tennessee Honey, Jack Daniel's Tennessee Fire, Jack Daniel's Tennessee Apple, Gentleman Jack, Jack Daniel's Single Barrel, Woodford Reserve, Old Forester, Coopers’ Craft, The GlenDronach, Benriach, Glenglassaugh, Slane, Herradura, el Jimador, New Mix, Korbel, Chambord, Fords Gin, Gin Mare, and Diplomático Rum.

Shares of Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.A) (BF.B) were down around 7% in premarket trading.

data.title: U.S. trade deficit narrows sharply in April
data.description:
The U.S.'s international trade in goods and services deficit narrowed to -$61.6B in April, compared with the -$118.1B expected and -$138.3B in March (revised from -$140.5B), according to data released by the U.S. Commerce Department on Thursday.

Exports were $289.4 billion, $8.3 billion more than March exports.

Imports were $351.0 billion, $68.4 billion less than March imports.

The April decrease in the goods and services deficit reflected a decrease in the goods deficit of $75.2 billion to $87.4 billion and an increase in the services surplus of $1.5 billion to $25.8 billion.

  • Labor Pains Without The Epidural
  • Weak Jobs And Rising Inflation Fuel Stagflation Fears
  • Trump signs travel ban for citizens from 12 countries, citing security concerns
  • Stocks end flat, Treasury yields slide as traders flock to bonds after weak data

data.title: Soluna mines 10 bitcoins in May
data.description:
Bitcoin mining and AI data centers developer Soluna Holdings (NASDAQ:SLNH) announced on Thursday the mining of 10 bitcoins in May, unchanged from the previous month.

The hashrate was 1,839 PH/s and 634 PH/s for hosted and self-mining hash rate, respectively.

Shares were +2.83% pre-market to $0.63.

data.title: Diginex to acquire Resulticks for $2B
data.description:
• Diginex (NASDAQ:DGNX) has signed a memorandum of understanding for a cash and share acquisition of Resulticks for $2B.
  • It will be paid for in three tranches- $1.4B in Diginex ordinary shares valued at $72 per share and subject to a 12-18 month lock-up, which shares will be issued at closing of the transaction, $100M in cash that is payable within 90 business days of the closing of the transaction and an earnout of up to $500M payable in Diginex ordinary shares valued at $72 per share and paid in 3 independent tranches subject to Resulticks attaining at least 75% of the below audited
  • DGNX +3.24% premarket to $47.37.
  • Source: Press Release
